fields.description:
## Summary
REBAR_MCP_READONLY=TRUE (or True/YES) silently disables the readonly gate — the membership test is case-sensitive.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD)
1. REBAR_MCP_READONLY=TRUE; build_server().
2. list_tools() -> all 14 write tools exposed.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: common truthy spellings enable the gate; an operator setting TRUE believes the server is read-only.
- Actual: mcp_server.py:115-116 tests membership in ("1","true","yes") without .lower(); TRUE fails open (the dangerous direction).

## Source
- src/rebar/mcp_server.py:115-116 (_readonly)
- src/rebar/mcp_server.py:271-273 — REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE uses the same parse; its failure direction is safe (stricter) but fix both for consistency.

## Fix direction
.strip().lower() before the membership test in one tiny _env_truthy helper used by both checks.

## Suggested RED test
Parametrize TRUE/True/YES/Yes/1/yes/true -> all readonly (write tools hidden); ""/0/no -> not readonly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Case-insensitive truthy parse for REBAR_MCP_READONLY and REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE
- [ ] RED parametrized test first
- [ ] Documented accepted values in README/CLAUDE.md
